questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司运行高度可用的 SFTP 服务。 SFTP 服务使用两个使用弹性 IP 地址运行的 Amazon EC2 Linux 实例来接受来自互联网上受信任 IP 源的流量。 SFTP 服务由附加到实例的共享存储提供支持。用户账户在 SFTP 服务器中作为 Linux 用户创建和管理。该公司想要一种能够提供高 IOPS 性能和高度可配置安全性的无服务器选项。该公司还希望保持对用户权限的控制。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a highly available SFTP service. The SFTP service uses two Amazon EC2 Linux instances that run with elastic IP addresses to accept traffic from trusted IP sources on the internet. The SFTP service is backed by shared storage that is attached to the instances. User accounts are created and managed as Linux users in the SFTP servers. The company wants a serverless option that provides high IOPS performance and highly configurable security. The company also wants to maintain control over user permissions. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 创建加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 卷。使用仅允许受信任 IP 地址的公共终端节点创建 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务。将 EBS 卷附加到 SFTP 服务端点。授予用户访问 SFTP 服务的权限。
A.en: Create an encrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with a public endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the EBS volume to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

B.zh: 创建加密的 Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) 卷。使用弹性 IP 地址和具有面向 Internet 访问权限的 VPC 终端节点创建 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务。将安全组附加到仅允许受信任 IP 地址的端点。将 EFS 卷附加到 SFTP 服务端点。授予用户访问 SFTP 服务的权限。
B.en: Create an encrypted Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) volume. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with elastic IP addresses and a VPC endpoint that has internet-facing access. Attach a security group to the endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the EFS volume to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

C.zh: 创建启用默认加密的 Amazon S3 存储桶。使用仅允许受信任 IP 地址的公共终端节点创建 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务。将 S3 存储桶附加到 SFTP 服务端点。授予用户访问 SFTP 服务的权限。
C.en: Create an Amazon S3 bucket with default encryption enabled. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with a public endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the S3 bucket to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

D.zh: 创建启用默认加密的 Amazon S3 存储桶。使用在私有子网中具有内部访问权限的 VPC 终端节点创建 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务。附加仅允许受信任 IP 地址的安全组。将 S3 存储桶附加到 SFTP 服务端点。授予用户访问 SFTP 服务的权限。
D.en: Create an Amazon S3 bucket with default encryption enabled. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with a VPC endpoint that has internal access in a private subnet. Attach a security group that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the S3 bucket to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：创建加密的 Amazon EBS 卷并使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务：Amazon EBS 是块存储，通常用于单个 EC2 实例，而不是共享存储。 AWS Transfer Family 无法直接附加 EBS 卷。此选项不满足高性能共享存储的要求，因此是一个错误。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Create an encrypted Amazon EBS volume and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon EBS is block storage and is typically used for individual EC2 instances, not shared storage. AWS Transfer Family cannot attach EBS volumes directly. This option does not meet the requirements for High Performance Shared Storage and is therefore an error.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：创建加密的 Amazon EFS 卷并使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务：Amazon EFS 是一种共享文件存储，支持高 IOPS 性能，适合多实例访问。 AWS Transfer Family可以通过VPC端点与EFS集成，以满足“高性能”和“安全”的要求。这个选项是正确的。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Create an encrypted Amazon EFS volume and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon EFS is a shared file storage that supports high IOPS performance and is suitable for multi-instance access. AWS Transfer Family can be integrated with EFS through VPC endpoints to meet the requirements of "high performance" and "security". This option is correct.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：创建 Amazon S3 存储桶并使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务：Amazon S3 是图像存储。虽然支持AWS Transfer Family，但不适合高IOPS场景。该选项不满足“高能量存储”的要求，因此是一个错误。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Create an Amazon S3 bucket and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon S3 is an image storage. Although it supports AWS Transfer Family, it is not suitable for high IOPS scenarios. This option does not meet the requirements for "high energy storage" and is therefore an error.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：创建 Amazon S3 存储桶并使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务（私有子网访问）：与选项 C 类似，Amazon S3 不适合高 IOPS 场景。虽然安全数据库配置正确，但存储类型不符合要求，因此出现错误。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Create an Amazon S3 bucket and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service (private subnet access): Similar to option C, Amazon S3 is not suitable for high IOPS scenarios. Although the security database is configured correctly, the storage type does not meet the requirements, hence the error.

answer_text.zh: B. 创建加密的 Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) 卷。使用弹性 IP 地址和具有面向 Internet 访问权限的 VPC 终端节点创建 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务。将安全组附加到仅允许受信任 IP 地址的端点。将 EFS 卷附加到 SFTP 服务端点。授予用户访问 SFTP 服务的权限。
answer_text.en: B. Create an encrypted Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) volume. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with elastic IP addresses and a VPC endpoint that has internet-facing access. Attach a security group to the endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the EFS volume to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (85%) Other。热门讨论与考点摘要：创建加密的 Amazon EFS 卷并使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服务：Amazon EFS 是一种共享文件存储，支持高 IOPS 性能，适合多实例访问。 AWS Transfer Family可以通过VPC端点与EFS集成，以满足“高性能”和“安全”的要求。这个选项是正确的。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (85%) Other. Popular discussion and key point summary: Create an encrypted Amazon EFS volume and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon EFS is a shared file storage that supports high IOPS performance and is suitable for multi-instance access. AWS Transfer Family can be integrated with EFS through VPC endpoints to meet the requirements of "high performance" and "security". This option is correct.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在 AWS 上开发新的机器学习 (ML) 模型解决方案。这些模型被开发为独立的微服务，在启动时从 Amazon S3 获取大约 1 GB 的模型数据并将数据加载到内存中。用户通过异步 API 访问模型。用户可以发送一个请求或一批请求，并指定结果应发送到何处。该公司为数百名用户提供模型。模型的使用模式是不规则的。某些型号可能会闲置数天或数周。其他模型可以一次接收数千个批次的请求。解决方案架构师应该推荐哪种设计来满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is developing a new machine learning (ML) model solution on AWS. The models are developed as independent microservices that fetch approximately 1 GB of model data from Amazon S3 at startup and load the data into memory. Users access the models through an asynchronous API. Users can send a request or a batch of requests and specify where the results should be sent. The company provides models to hundreds of users. The usage patterns for the models are irregular. Some models could be unused for days or weeks. Other models could receive batches of thousands of requests at a time. Which design should a solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 将来自 API 的请求定向到网络负载均衡器 (NLB)。将模型部署为 NLB 调用的 AWS Lambda 函数。
A.en: Direct the requests from the API to a Network Load Balancer (NLB). Deploy the models as AWS Lambda functions that are invoked by the NLB.

B.zh: 将来自 API 的请求定向到应用程序负载均衡器 (ALB)。将模型部署为从 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列读取的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 服务。使用 AWS App Mesh 根据 SQS 队列大小扩展 ECS 集群的实例。
B.en: Direct the requests from the API to an Application Load Balancer (ALB). Deploy the models as Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) services that read from an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Use AWS App Mesh to scale the instances of the ECS cluster based on the SQS queue size.

C.zh: 将来自 API 的请求定向到 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。将模型部署为由 SQS 事件调用的 AWS Lambda 函数。使用 AWS Auto Scaling 根据 SQS 队列大小增加 Lambda 函数的 vCPU 数量。
C.en: Direct the requests from the API into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Deploy the models as AWS Lambda functions that are invoked by SQS events. Use AWS Auto Scaling to increase the number of vCPUs for the Lambda functions based on the SQS queue size.

D.zh: 将来自 API 的请求定向到 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。将模型部署为从队列中读取的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 服务。根据队列大小为集群和服务副本在 Amazon ECS 上启用 AWS Auto Scaling。
D.en: Direct the requests from the API into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Deploy the models as Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) services that read from the queue. Enable AWS Auto Scaling on Amazon ECS for both the cluster and copies of the service based on the queue size.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Lambda 函数不适合处理 1GB 模型数据加载到内存的场景，因为 Lambda 的内存和执行时间（最多 15 分钟）有限。此外，NLB 通常用于处理 TCP/UDP 流量，而不是异步 API 请求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The Lambda function is not suitable for handling scenarios where 1GB of model data is loaded into memory because Lambda has limited memory and execution time (maximum 15 minutes). Additionally, NLB is typically used to handle TCP/UDP traffic rather than asynchronous API requests.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然 ALB 和 ECS 的组合是有效的，但使用 AWS App Mesh 来扩展 ECS 实例并不是最佳选择。 AWS App Mesh主要用于服务间通信而不是直接扩展ECS实例。另外，这种场景下不需要使用ALB，因为请求已经通过SQS队列异步处理。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: While the combination of ALB and ECS is effective, using AWS App Mesh to scale ECS instances is not the best option. AWS App Mesh is mainly used for inter-service communication rather than directly extending ECS ​​instances. In addition, the use of ALB is not necessary in this scenario because the request is already processed asynchronously through the SQS queue.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Lambda 函数仍然不适合处理 1GB 模型数据加载到内存的场景。 Lambda 的内存和执行时间限制使其非结构化以满足需求。此外，Lambda 直接通过 Auto Scaling 增加 vCPU 数量，无需剥离。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Lambda functions are still not suitable for handling scenarios where 1GB of model data is loaded into memory. Lambda's memory and execution time constraints make it unstructured to meet demand. In addition, Lambda increases the number of vCPUs directly through Auto Scaling without stripping.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：此选项使用 SQS 队列处理异步 API 请求并通过 ECS 服务加载模型数据。 ECS支持大规模数据加载到内存的场景，并且可以通过AWS Auto Scaling根据队列大小动态扩展服务实例和集群容量。这种设计满足了不规则的使用模式和高并发请求的需求。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option uses an SQS queue to handle asynchronous API requests and loads model data through the ECS service. ECS supports scenarios where large-scale data is loaded into memory, and service instances and cluster capacity can be dynamically expanded based on queue size through AWS Auto Scaling. This design meets the needs of irregular usage patterns and high concurrent requests.

answer_text.zh: D. 将来自 API 的请求定向到 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。将模型部署为从队列中读取的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 服务。根据队列大小为集群和服务副本在 Amazon ECS 上启用 AWS Auto Scaling。
answer_text.en: D. Direct the requests from the API into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Deploy the models as Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) services that read from the queue. Enable AWS Auto Scaling on Amazon ECS for both the cluster and copies of the service based on the queue size.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：此选项使用 SQS 队列处理异步 API 请求并通过 ECS 服务加载模型数据。 ECS支持大规模数据加载到内存的场景，并且可以通过AWS Auto Scaling根据队列大小动态扩展服务实例和集群容量。这种设计满足了不规则的使用模式和高并发请求的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option uses an SQS queue to handle asynchronous API requests and loads model data through the ECS service. ECS supports scenarios where large-scale data is loaded into memory, and service instances and cluster capacity can be dynamically expanded based on queue size through AWS Auto Scaling. This design meets the needs of irregular usage patterns and high concurrent requests.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解决方案架构师希望使用以下 JSON 文本作为基于身份的策略来授予特定权限：解决方案架构师可以将此策略附加到哪些 IAM 委托人？ （选择两个。）
question_text.en: A solutions architect wants to use the following JSON text as an identity-based policy to grant specific permissions: Which IAM principals can the solutions architect attach this policy to? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 角色
A.en: Role

B.zh: 集团
B.en: Group

C.zh: 组织机构
C.en: Organization

D.zh: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 资源
D.en: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) resource

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：角色：正确。 IAM 角色是可以附加身份策略的主体。身份策略可以直接为角色授予权限。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Role: Correct. An IAM role is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. Identity policies can directly grant permissions to roles.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：组：正确。 IAM 用户组是可以附加身份策略的委托人。用户组中的所有用户都继承该组附加的权限。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Group: Correct. An IAM user group is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. All users in a user group inherit the permissions attached to the group.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：组织：错误。 AWS Organizations 是一项管理多个 AWS 账户的服务，无法直接附加身份策略。策略需要通过服务控制策略 (SCP) 进行管理，而不是身份策略。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Organization: Error. AWS Organizations is a service that manages multiple AWS accounts and cannot directly attach identity policies. Policies need to be managed through Service Control Policies (SCPs), not identity policies.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 资源：错误。 Amazon ECS 资源属于服务资源，不能直接附加身份策略。资源权限通常通过资源策略或角色进行管理。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) resource: Error. Amazon ECS resources are service resources and cannot be directly attached with identity policies. Resource permissions are typically managed through resource policies or roles.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon EC2 资源：错误。 Amazon EC2 资源属于服务资源，不能直接附加身份策略。资源权限通常通过资源策略或角色进行管理。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon EC2 resource: Error. Amazon EC2 resources are service resources and cannot be directly attached with identity policies. Resource permissions are typically managed through resource policies or roles.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：AB (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：角色：正确。 IAM 角色是可以附加身份策略的主体。身份策略可以直接为角色授予权限。；组：正确。 IAM 用户组是可以附加身份策略的委托人。用户组中的所有用户都继承该组附加的权限。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: AB (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Role: Correct. An IAM role is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. Identity policies can directly grant permissions to roles.; Group: Correct. An IAM user group is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. All users in a user group inherit the permissions attached to the group.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在 Amazon EC2 按需实例上运行自定义应用程序。该应用程序的前端节点需要每周 7 天、每天 24 小时运行，而后端节点则根据工作负载仅需要运行很短的时间。后端节点的数量在一天中会有所不同。公司需要根据工作负载横向扩展和扩展更多实例。哪种解决方案能够最具成本效益地满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is running a custom application on Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances. The application has frontend nodes that need to run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week and backend nodes that need to run only for a short time based on workload. The number of backend nodes varies during the day. The company needs to scale out and scale in more instances based on workload. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 对前端节点使用预留实例。使用 AWS Fargate 作为后端节点。
A.en: Use Reserved Instances for the frontend nodes. Use AWS Fargate for the backend nodes.

B.zh: 对前端节点使用预留实例。使用 竞价型实例作为后端节点。
B.en: Use Reserved Instances for the frontend nodes. Use Spot Instances for the backend nodes.

C.zh: 使用 竞价型实例作为前端节点。对后端节点使用预留实例。
C.en: Use Spot Instances for the frontend nodes. Use Reserved Instances for the backend nodes.

D.zh: 使用 竞价型实例作为前端节点。使用 AWS Fargate 作为后端节点。
D.en: Use Spot Instances for the frontend nodes. Use AWS Fargate for the backend nodes.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：技术分析：预留实例适合长时间运行的工作负载，因此为此前端节点使用预留实例是正确的选择。错误点：AWS Fargate主要用于容器化工作负载，但标题没有提及后端节点是容器化应用程序。此外，Fargate 的成本通常高于 竞价型实例，因此它不是最具成本效益的选择。结论：这个选项是错误的。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Technical analysis: Reserved Instances are suitable for long-running workloads, so using Reserved Instances for this front-end node is the right choice. Point of error: AWS Fargate is mainly used for containerized workloads, but the title does not mention that the backend nodes are containerized applications. In addition, the cost of Fargate is usually higher than that of Spot Instances, so it is not the most cost-effective option. Conclusion: This option is wrong.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：技术分析：预留实例适合长时间运行的工作负载，因此为此前端节点使用预留实例是正确的选择。技术分析：竞价型实例适合短期、可中断的工作负载，且成本最低，因此该后端节点使用 竞价型实例是最具成本效益的选择。结论：该选项正确。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Technical analysis: Reserved Instances are suitable for long-running workloads, so using Reserved Instances for this front-end node is the right choice. Technical analysis: Spot Instances are suitable for short-term, interruptible workloads and have the lowest cost, so using Spot Instances for this backend node is the most cost-effective option. Conclusion: This option is correct.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：错误点：前端节点需要24/7运行，Spot实例可能会中断，不适合该场景。错误点：后端节点是短期工作负载。使用预留实例会导致资源消耗和高成本。结论：这个选项是错误的。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Error point: The front-end node needs to run 24/7, Spot Instances may be interrupted, and it is not suitable for this scenario. Error point: Backend nodes are short-term workloads. Using Reserved Instances will lead to resource consumption and high costs. Conclusion: This option is wrong.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：错误点：前端节点需要24/7运行，Spot实例可能会中断，不适合该场景。错误点：AWS Fargate 成本更高，而且问题没有提到后端节点是容器化工作负载。结论：这个选项是错误的。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Error point: The front-end node needs to run 24/7, Spot Instances may be interrupted, and it is not suitable for this scenario. Points of error: AWS Fargate costs more, and the question does not mention that the backend nodes are containerized workloads. Conclusion: This option is wrong.

answer_text.zh: B. 对前端节点使用预留实例。使用 竞价型实例作为后端节点。
answer_text.en: B. Use Reserved Instances for the frontend nodes. Use Spot Instances for the backend nodes.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (62%) A (38%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：技术分析：预留实例适合长时间运行的工作负载，因此为此前端节点使用预留实例是正确的选择。技术分析：竞价型实例适合短期、可中断的工作负载，且成本最低，因此该后端节点使用 竞价型实例是最具成本效益的选择。结论：该选项正确。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (62%) A (38%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Technical analysis: Reserved Instances are suitable for long-running workloads, so using Reserved Instances for this front-end node is the right choice. Technical analysis: Spot Instances are suitable for short-term, interruptible workloads and have the lowest cost, so using Spot Instances for this backend node is the most cost-effective option. Conclusion: This option is correct.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用高块存储容量在本地运行其工作负载。公司每天每秒的峰值输入输出交易不超过15,000 IOPS。该公司希望将工作负载迁移到 Amazon EC2，并独立于存储容量配置磁盘性能。哪种 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 卷类型能够最经济高效地满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company uses high block storage capacity to runs its workloads on premises. The company's daily peak input and output transactions per second are not more than 15,000 IOPS. The company wants to migrate the workloads to Amazon EC2 and to provision disk performance independent of storage capacity. Which Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume type will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: GP2卷类型
A.en: GP2 volume type

B.zh: io2 卷类型
B.en: io2 volume type

C.zh: GP3卷类型
C.en: GP3 volume type

D.zh: io1 卷类型
D.en: io1 volume type

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：GP2 卷类型的性能与存储容量相关，IOPS 随着存储容量的增加而增加。虽然GP2可以支持高IOPS，但其性能不能独立于存储容量进行配置，因此不能满足问题要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The performance of GP2 volume types is tied to storage capacity, with IOPS increasing as storage capacity increases. Although GP2 can support high IOPS, its performance cannot be configured independently of storage capacity, so it does not meet the question requirements.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：io2卷类型提供高性能和高耐用性，支持独立配置IOPS，但成本较高。问题要求“最具成本效益”的解决方案，因此io2不是最佳选择。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The io2 volume type provides high performance and high durability and supports independent configuration of IOPS, but the cost is higher. The question asks for the "most cost-effective" solution, so io2 is not the best choice.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：GP3 卷类型支持独立配置 IOPS 和吞吐量，无论存储容量如何。 GP3 的成本低于 io1 和 io2，并且可以处理 15,000 IOPS，使其成为最具成本效益的选择。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: The GP3 volume type supports independent configuration of IOPS and throughput, regardless of storage capacity. GP3 costs less than io1 and io2 and can handle 15,000 IOPS, making it the most cost-effective option.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：io1卷类型支持高性能和独立配置的IOPS，但价格更昂贵。与io2类似，io1不满足“最具成本效益”的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The io1 volume type supports high performance and independently configured IOPS, but is more expensive. Similar to io2, io1 does not meet the "most cost effective" requirement.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (96%) 4%。热门讨论与考点摘要：GP3 卷类型支持独立配置 IOPS 和吞吐量，无论存储容量如何。 GP3 的成本低于 io1 和 io2，并且可以处理 15,000 IOPS，使其成为最具成本效益的选择。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (96%) 4%. Popular discussion and key point summary: The GP3 volume type supports independent configuration of IOPS and throughput, regardless of storage capacity. GP3 costs less than io1 and io2 and can handle 15,000 IOPS, making it the most cost-effective option.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 公司需要存储来自其医疗保健应用程序的数据。应用程序的数据经常更改。新法规要求对所有级别的存储数据进行审计访问。该公司将应用程序托管在存储容量不足的本地基础设施上。解决方案架构师必须将现有数据安全地迁移到 AWS，同时满足新法规。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to store data from its healthcare application. The application’s data frequently changes. A new regulation requires audit access at all levels of the stored data. The company hosts the application on an on-premises infrastructure that is running out of storage capacity. A solutions architect must securely migrate the existing data to AWS while satisfying the new regulation. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS DataSync 将现有数据移动到 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 记录数据事件。
A.en: Use AWS DataSync to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log data events.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Snowcone 将现有数据移动到 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 记录管理事件。
B.en: Use AWS Snowcone to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log management events.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 将现有数据移动到 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 记录数据事件。
C.en: Use Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log data events.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Storage Gateway 将现有数据移动到 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 记录管理事件。
D.en: Use AWS Storage Gateway to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log management events.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS DataSync 是专门为高效、安全地将数据迁移到 AWS 而设计的服务，适合处理频繁变化的数据。 Amazon S3 是一种高度可用且高度持久的存储服务，适合存储医疗现场数据。 AWS CloudTrail 可以记录数据事件以满足审计需求。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS DataSync is a service specially designed to migrate data to AWS efficiently and securely, and is suitable for handling frequently changing data. Amazon S3 is a highly available and highly durable storage service suitable for storing medical field data. AWS CloudTrail can record data events to meet auditing needs.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Snowcone适合小规模数据迁移，但标题没有提及数据的大小，并且Snowcone不适合频繁变化的数据。 CloudTrail的管理事件记录的是账户相关的操作，而不是数据相关的操作，无法满足审计要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Snowcone is suitable for small-scale data migration, but the title does not mention the size of the data, and Snowcone is not suitable for frequently changing data. CloudTrail's management events record account-related operations, not data-related operations, and cannot meet audit requirements.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration适合加速跨区域数据上传，但标题没有提及跨区域场景。虽然 CloudTrail 可以记录数据事件，但 Transfer Acceleration 并不是最好的迁移工具。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration is suitable for accelerating cross-region data upload, but the title does not mention cross-region scenarios. While CloudTrail can log data events, Transfer Acceleration is not the best migration tool.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Storage Gateway适用于混合云存储场景，但问题是需要数据迁移且不允许访问本地数据。 CloudTrail的管理事件无法满足数据审计需求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Storage Gateway is suitable for hybrid cloud storage scenarios, but the problem requires data migration and does not allow local data to be accessed. CloudTrail's management events cannot meet data audit needs.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 AWS DataSync 将现有数据移动到 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 记录数据事件。
answer_text.en: A. Use AWS DataSync to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log data events.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (69%) D (31%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS DataSync 是专门为高效、安全地将数据迁移到 AWS 而设计的服务，适合处理频繁变化的数据。 Amazon S3 是一种高度可用且高度持久的存储服务，适合存储医疗现场数据。 AWS CloudTrail 可以记录数据事件以满足审计需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (69%) D (31%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS DataSync is a service specially designed to migrate data to AWS efficiently and securely, and is suitable for handling frequently changing data. Amazon S3 is a highly available and highly durable storage service suitable for storing medical field data. AWS CloudTrail can record data events to meet auditing needs.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解决方案架构师正在使用 MySQL 数据库实现复杂的 Java 应用程序。 Java 应用程序必须部署在 Apache Tomcat 上，并且必须具有高可用性。解决方案架构师应该怎样做才能满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A solutions architect is implementing a complex Java application with a MySQL database. The Java application must be deployed on Apache Tomcat and must be highly available. What should the solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在 AWS Lambda 中部署应用程序。配置 Amazon API Gateway API 以与 Lambda 函数连接。
A.en: Deploy the application in AWS Lambda. Configure an Amazon API Gateway API to connect with the Lambda functions.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 部署应用程序。配置负载均衡环境和滚动部署策略。
B.en: Deploy the application by using AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Configure a load-balanced environment and a rolling deployment policy.

C.zh: 将数据库迁移到 Amazon ElastiCache。配置 ElastiCache 安全组以允许从应用程序进行访问。
C.en: Migrate the database to Amazon ElastiCache. Configure the ElastiCache security group to allow access from the application.

D.zh: 启动 Amazon EC2 实例。在 EC2 实例上安装 MySQL 服务器。在服务器上配置应用程序。创建 AMI。使用 AMI 创建带有 Auto Scaling 组的启动模板。
D.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance. Install a MySQL server on the EC2 instance. Configure the application on the server. Create an AMI. Use the AMI to create a launch template with an Auto Scaling group.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Lambda适合无服务器架构，但问题显然需要部署复杂的Java应用程序。这种应用程序通常需要持久化的运行环境（例如Tomcat），而Lambda不适合运行复杂的Java应用程序。另外，Lambda无法直接满足高可用和负载均衡的需求，连接MySQL数据库需要额外的配置。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Lambda is suitable for serverless architecture, but the question clearly requires the deployment of a complex Java application. This application usually requires a persistent running environment (such as Tomcat), and Lambda is not suitable for running complex Java applications. In addition, Lambda cannot directly meet the needs of high availability and load balancing, and the connection to the MySQL database requires additional configuration.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Elastic Beanstalk 是一项托管服务，支持 Java 应用程序的部署并自动配置负载平衡和高可用性环境。滚动部署策略保证应用程序更新不会中断服务，满足高可用性要求。 Elastic Beanstalk还支持Tomcat作为运行环境，非常适合问题中的需求。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Elastic Beanstalk is a managed service that supports the deployment of Java applications and automatically configures load balancing and high-availability environments. The rolling deployment strategy ensures that application updates will not interrupt service and meet high availability requirements. Elastic Beanstalk also supports Tomcat as the operating environment, which is very suitable for the needs in the question.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon ElastiCache 是一种内存缓存服务，适合加速数据库查询或存储临时数据，但它无法取代 MySQL 数据库。该问题需要MySQL数据库，而不是缓存服务，因此该选项不满足要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon ElastiCache is an in-memory cache service suitable for accelerating database queries or storing temporary data, but it cannot replace the MySQL database. The question requires a MySQL database, not a cache service, so this option does not meet the requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：这种方式虽然可以实现高可用，但需要手动配置和管理EC2实例、负载均衡器、Auto Scaling组等，复杂度较高。 Elastic Beanstalk 可以自动处理这些任务，因此更适合问题所需要的场景。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although this method can achieve high availability, it requires manual configuration and management of EC2 instances, load balancers, Auto Scaling groups, etc., which is highly complex. Elastic Beanstalk can handle these tasks automatically, so it is more suitable for the scenario required by the question.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 部署应用程序。配置负载均衡环境和滚动部署策略。
answer_text.en: B. Deploy the application by using AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Configure a load-balanced environment and a rolling deployment policy.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Elastic Beanstalk 是一项托管服务，支持 Java 应用程序的部署并自动配置负载平衡和高可用性环境。滚动部署策略保证应用程序更新不会中断服务，满足高可用性要求。 Elastic Beanstalk还支持Tomcat作为运行环境，非常适合问题中的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Elastic Beanstalk is a managed service that supports the deployment of Java applications and automatically configures load balancing and high-availability environments. The rolling deployment strategy ensures that application updates will not interrupt service and meet high availability requirements. Elastic Beanstalk also supports Tomcat as the operating environment, which is very suitable for the needs in the question.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 无服务器应用程序使用 Amazon API Gateway、AWS Lambda 和 Amazon DynamoDB。 Lambda 函数需要读取和写入 DynamoDB 表的权限。哪种解决方案可以让 Lambda 函数最安全地访问 DynamoDB 表？
question_text.en: A serverless application uses Amazon API Gateway, AWS Lambda, and Amazon DynamoDB. The Lambda function needs permissions to read and write to the DynamoDB table. Which solution will give the Lambda function access to the DynamoDB table MOST securely?

A.zh: 创建一个能够以编程方式访问 Lambda 函数的 IAM 用户。向用户附加允许对 DynamoDB 表进行读写访问的策略。将 access_key_id 和 Secret_access_key 参数存储为 Lambda 环境变量的一部分。确保其他 AWS 用户没有对 Lambda 函数配置的读写访问权限。
A.en: Create an IAM user with programmatic access to the Lambda function. Attach a policy to the user that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Store the access_key_id and secret_access_key parameters as part of the Lambda environment variables. Ensure that other AWS users do not have read and write access to the Lambda function configuration.

B.zh: 创建一个 IAM 角色，其中包含 Lambda 作为可信服务。将策略附加到允许对 DynamoDB 表进行读写访问的角色。更新 Lambda 函数的配置以使用新角色作为执行角色。
B.en: Create an IAM role that includes Lambda as a trusted service. Attach a policy to the role that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Update the configuration of the Lambda function to use the new role as the execution role.

C.zh: 创建一个能够以编程方式访问 Lambda 函数的 IAM 用户。向用户附加允许对 DynamoDB 表进行读写访问的策略。将 access_key_id 和 Secret_access_key 参数作为安全字符串参数存储在 AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store 中。更新 Lambda 函数代码以在连接到 DynamoDB 表之前检索安全字符串参数。
C.en: Create an IAM user with programmatic access to the Lambda function. Attach a policy to the user that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Store the access_key_id and secret_access_key parameters in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store as secure string parameters. Update the Lambda function code to retrieve the secure string parameters before connecting to the DynamoDB table.

D.zh: 创建一个 IAM 角色，其中包含 DynamoDB 作为可信服务。将策略附加到允许从 Lambda 函数进行读写访问的角色。更新 Lambda 函数的代码以作为执行角色附加到新角色。
D.en: Create an IAM role that includes DynamoDB as a trusted service. Attach a policy to the role that allows read and write access from the Lambda function. Update the code of the Lambda function to attach to the new role as an execution role.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用 IAM 用户并将 access_key_id 和 Secret_access_key 存储在 Lambda 环境变量中存在安全风险。环境变量可能会意外暴露，从而违反安全最佳实践。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: There is a security risk in using an IAM user and storing access_key_id and secret_access_key in Lambda environment variables. Environment variables can be accidentally exposed, violating security best practices.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：这是AWS推荐的最佳实践。通过将 IAM 角色分配给 Lambda 函数，Lambda 可以安全地访问 DynamoDB，而无需显式存储凭证。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This is a best practice recommended by AWS. By assigning an IAM role to a Lambda function, Lambda can securely access DynamoDB without explicitly storing credentials.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：尽管使用 AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store 存储凭证比直接将凭证存储在环境变量中更安全，但它仍然不如使用 IAM 角色安全。 IAM 用户的凭证可能会受到泄露。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although using AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store to store credentials is more secure than storing them directly in environment variables, it is still not as secure as using IAM roles. An IAM user's credentials may be compromised.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：IAM 角色的信任关系应该是 Lambda，而不是 DynamoDB。 DynamoDB 不需要成为可信服务。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The trust relationship for the IAM role should be Lambda, not DynamoDB. DynamoDB does not need to be a trusted service.

answer_text.zh: B. 创建一个 IAM 角色，其中包含 Lambda 作为可信服务。将策略附加到允许对 DynamoDB 表进行读写访问的角色。更新 Lambda 函数的配置以使用新角色作为执行角色。
answer_text.en: B. Create an IAM role that includes Lambda as a trusted service. Attach a policy to the role that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Update the configuration of the Lambda function to use the new role as the execution role.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：这是AWS推荐的最佳实践。通过将 IAM 角色分配给 Lambda 函数，Lambda 可以安全地访问 DynamoDB，而无需显式存储凭证。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This is a best practice recommended by AWS. By assigning an IAM role to a Lambda function, Lambda can securely access DynamoDB without explicitly storing credentials.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 以下 IAM 策略附加到 IAM 组。这是应用于该组的唯一策略。该策略对群组成员的有效 IAM 权限有哪些？
question_text.en: The following IAM policy is attached to an IAM group. This is the only policy applied to the group. What are the effective IAM permissions of this policy for group members?

A.zh: 允许组成员在 us-east-1 区域内执行任何 Amazon EC2 操作。不应用“允许”权限之后的语句。
A.en: Group members are permitted any Amazon EC2 action within the us-east-1 Region. Statements after the Allow permission are not applied.

B.zh: 组成员在 us-east-1 区域中被拒绝任何 Amazon EC2 权限，除非他们使用多重身份验证 (MFA) 登录。
B.en: Group members are denied any Amazon EC2 permissions in the us-east-1 Region unless they are logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA).

C.zh: 使用多重身份验证 (MFA) 登录时，组成员可以拥有所有区域的 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 权限。允许组成员执行任何其他 Amazon EC2 操作。
C.en: Group members are allowed the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances permissions for all Regions when logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA). Group members are permitted any other Amazon EC2 action.

D.zh: 仅当使用多重身份验证 (MFA) 登录时，组成员才可以拥有 us-east-1 区域的 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 权限。允许组成员在 us-east-1 区域内执行任何其他 Amazon EC2 操作。
D.en: Group members are allowed the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances permissions for the us-east-1 Region only when logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA). Group members are permitted any other Amazon EC2 action within the us-east-1 Region.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：IAM 策略的评估顺序是所有语句都会被评估，拒绝（Deny）权限优先于允许（Allow）。因此，后续的deny语句会影响最终的权限。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The evaluation order of IAM policies is that all statements will be evaluated, and deny (Deny) permissions take precedence over allow (Allow). Therefore, subsequent deny statements affect the final permissions.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：us-east-1 区域内的策略中明确允许所有 EC2 操作 (ec2:*)，当不使用 MFA 时，仅拒绝特定操作（ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances）。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: All EC2 operations (ec2:*) are explicitly allowed in the policy within the us-east-1 region, only specific operations (ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances) are denied when MFA is not used.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：策略中的allow语句明确将操作范围限制在us-east-1区域，因此其他区域的权限不适用。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The allow statement in the policy explicitly limits the scope of the operation to the us-east-1 region, so permissions from other regions do not apply.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：该策略的第一个语句允许所有 EC2 操作在 us-east-1 区域内执行。第二条语句拒绝 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 操作，除非用户使用 MFA。所以最终的权威符合描述。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: The first statement of the policy allows all EC2 operations to be executed within the us-east-1 region. The second statement denies the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances operations unless the user uses MFA. So the final authority fits the description.

answer_text.zh: D. 仅当使用多重身份验证 (MFA) 登录时，组成员才可以拥有 us-east-1 区域的 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 权限。允许组成员在 us-east-1 区域内执行任何其他 Amazon EC2 操作。
answer_text.en: D. Group members are allowed the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances permissions for the us-east-1 Region only when logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA). Group members are permitted any other Amazon EC2 action within the us-east-1 Region.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (90%) 10%。热门讨论与考点摘要：该策略的第一个语句允许所有 EC2 操作在 us-east-1 区域内执行。第二条语句拒绝 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 操作，除非用户使用 MFA。所以最终的权威符合描述。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (90%) 10%. Popular discussion and key point summary: The first statement of the policy allows all EC2 operations to be executed within the us-east-1 region. The second statement denies the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances operations unless the user uses MFA. So the final authority fits the description.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家制造公司拥有将 .csv 文件上传到 Amazon S3 存储桶的机器传感器。这些 .csv 文件必须转换为图像，并且必须尽快提供以自动生成图形报告。图像在 1 个月后变得无关紧要，但必须保留 .csv 文件，以便每年训练两次机器学习 (ML) 模型。机器学习培训和审核是提前几周计划的。哪种步骤组合能够最经济有效地满足这些要求？ （选择两个。）
question_text.en: A manufacturing company has machine sensors that upload .csv files to an Amazon S3 bucket. These .csv files must be converted into images and must be made available as soon as possible for the automatic generation of graphical reports. The images become irrelevant after 1 month, but the .csv files must be kept to train machine learning (ML) models twice a year. The ML trainings and audits are planned weeks in advance. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 启动 Amazon EC2 竞价型实例，该实例每小时下载 .csv 文件、生成图像文件并将图像上传到 S3 存储桶。
A.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 Spot Instance that downloads the .csv files every hour, generates the image files, and uploads the images to the S3 bucket.

B.zh: 设计一个 AWS Lambda 函数，将 .csv 文件转换为图像并将图像存储在 S3 存储桶中。上传 .csv 文件时调用 Lambda 函数。
B.en: Design an AWS Lambda function that converts the .csv files into images and stores the images in the S3 bucket. Invoke the Lambda function when a .csv file is uploaded.

C.zh: 为 S3 存储桶中的 .csv 文件和图像文件创建 S3 生命周期规则。上传 1 天后，将 .csv 文件从 S3 Standard 转换到 S3 Glacier。图像文件将在 30 天后过期。
C.en: Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier 1 day after they are uploaded. Expire the image files after 30 days.

D.zh: 为 S3 存储桶中的 .csv 文件和图像文件创建 S3 生命周期规则。上传 1 天后，将 .csv 文件从 S3 标准转换为 S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA)。图像文件将在 30 天后过期。
D.en: Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA) 1 day after they are uploaded. Expire the image files after 30 days.

E.zh: 为 S3 存储桶中的 .csv 文件和图像文件创建 S3 生命周期规则。上传 1 天后，将 .csv 文件从 S3 标准转换为 S3 标准-不频繁访问 (S3 标准-IA)。将图像文件保存在低冗余存储 (RRS) 中。
E.en: Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) 1 day after they are uploaded. Keep the image files in Reduced Redundancy Storage (RRS).

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：EC2 Spot Instance虽然成本较低，但需要手动管理实例的生命周期，无法保证实例的持续可用性。相比之下，AWS Lambda更适合事件驱动的任务处理。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although the cost of EC2 Spot Instance is low, it requires manual management of the life cycle of the instance and cannot guarantee the continuous availability of the instance. In comparison, AWS Lambda is more suitable for event-driven task processing.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Lambda 是一种无服务器计算服务，支持事件驱动架构，可以在文件上传到 S3 时自动触发处理任务。 Lambda的费用是按照使用量计费的，非常适合这种场景。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports event-driven architecture and can automatically trigger processing tasks when files are uploaded to S3. Lambda's cost is billed based on usage, which is very suitable for this scenario.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：S3生命周期规则可以自动管理文件的存储类型和过期时间。将 .csv 文件移动到 S3 Glacier 可以显着降低存储成本，并且图像文件会在 30 天后过期。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: S3 Lifecycle rules can automatically manage the storage type and expiration time of files. Moving .csv files to S3 Glacier can significantly reduce storage costs, and image files expire after 30 days.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：S3 One Zone-IA虽然成本相对较低，但可用性不高。数据存储在单一可用区，存在数据丢失的风险。相比之下，S3 Glacier更适合长期存储，成本也更低。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although S3 One Zone-IA is relatively low-cost, it does not have high availability. Data is stored in a single availability zone, and there is a risk of data loss. In comparison, S3 Glacier is more suitable for long-term storage and has lower cost.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 已弃用减少冗余存储 (RRS)，因此不建议使用。 S3 Standard-IA虽然适合不经常访问的数据，但其成本高于S3 Glacier，不符合成本优化的要求。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Reduced Redundancy Storage (RRS) has been deprecated by AWS and is not recommended. Although S3 Standard-IA is suitable for infrequently accessed data, its cost is higher than S3 Glacier, which does not meet the requirements for cost optimization.

answer_text.zh: B. 设计一个 AWS Lambda 函数，将 .csv 文件转换为图像并将图像存储在 S3 存储桶中。上传 .csv 文件时调用 Lambda 函数。；C. 为 S3 存储桶中的 .csv 文件和图像文件创建 S3 生命周期规则。上传 1 天后，将 .csv 文件从 S3 Standard 转换到 S3 Glacier。图像文件将在 30 天后过期。
answer_text.en: B. Design an AWS Lambda function that converts the .csv files into images and stores the images in the S3 bucket. Invoke the Lambda function when a .csv file is uploaded.; C. Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier 1 day after they are uploaded. Expire the image files after 30 days.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BC (90%) 10%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Lambda 是一种无服务器计算服务，支持事件驱动架构，可以在文件上传到 S3 时自动触发处理任务。 Lambda的费用是按照使用量计费的，非常适合这种场景。；S3生命周期规则可以自动管理文件的存储类型和过期时间。将 .csv 文件移动到 S3 Glacier 可以显着降低存储成本，并且图像文件会在 30 天后过期。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BC (90%) 10%. Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports event-driven architecture and can automatically trigger processing tasks when files are uploaded to S3. Lambda's cost is billed based on usage, which is very suitable for this scenario.; S3 Lifecycle rules can automatically manage the storage type and expiration time of files. Moving .csv files to S3 Glacier can significantly reduce storage costs, and image files expire after 30 days.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司开发了一款新的视频游戏作为网络应用程序。该应用程序采用 VPC 中的三层架构，数据库层为 Amazon RDS for MySQL。多名选手将同时在线比赛。该游戏的开发人员希望近乎实时地显示前 10 名记分牌，并提供停止和恢复游戏的能力，同时保留当前分数。解决方案架构师应该怎样做才能满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company has developed a new video game as a web application. The application is in a three-tier architecture in a VPC with Amazon RDS for MySQL in the database layer. Several players will compete concurrently online. The game’s developers want to display a top-10 scoreboard in near-real time and offer the ability to stop and restore the game while preserving the current scores. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 设置 Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached 集群来缓存要显示的 Web 应用程序的分数。
A.en: Set up an Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached cluster to cache the scores for the web application to display.

B.zh: 设置 Amazon ElastiCache for Redis 集群来计算和缓存要显示的 Web 应用程序的分数。
B.en: Set up an Amazon ElastiCache for Redis cluster to compute and cache the scores for the web application to display.

C.zh: 将 Amazon CloudFront 分配放置在 Web 应用程序前面，以将记分板缓存在应用程序的一部分中。
C.en: Place an Amazon CloudFront distribution in front of the web application to cache the scoreboard in a section of the application.

D.zh: 在 Amazon RDS for MySQL 上创建只读副本以运行查询来计算记分牌并向 Web 应用程序提供读取流量。
D.en: Create a read replica on Amazon RDS for MySQL to run queries to compute the scoreboard and serve the read traffic to the web application.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Memcached无法满足计算需求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Memcached cannot meet the computing needs.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Redis完全满足题目要求，包括计算、缓存和持久化功能。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Redis fully meets the question requirements, including calculation, caching and persistence functions.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：CloudFront 无法处理动态计算需求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: CloudFront cannot handle dynamic computing needs.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：数据库查询效率低，无法满足实时性要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Database query efficiency is low and cannot meet real-time requirements.

answer_text.zh: B. 设置 Amazon ElastiCache for Redis 集群来计算和缓存要显示的 Web 应用程序的分数。
answer_text.en: B. Set up an Amazon ElastiCache for Redis cluster to compute and cache the scores for the web application to display.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (92%) 8%。热门讨论与考点摘要：Redis完全满足题目要求，包括计算、缓存和持久化功能。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (92%) 8%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Redis fully meets the question requirements, including calculation, caching and persistence functions.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家电子商务公司希望使用机器学习 (ML) 算法来构建和训练模型。该公司将使用这些模型来可视化复杂的场景并检测客户数据的趋势。架构团队希望将其 ML 模型与报告平台集成，以分析增强数据并直接在其商业智能仪表板中使用数据。哪种解决方案能够以最少的运营开销满足这些要求？
question_text.en: An ecommerce company wants to use machine learning (ML) algorithms to build and train models. The company will use the models to visualize complex scenarios and to detect trends in customer data. The architecture team wants to integrate its ML models with a reporting platform to analyze the augmented data and use the data directly in its business intelligence dashboards. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Glue 创建 ML 转换来构建和训练模型。使用 Amazon OpenSearch Service 可视化数据。
A.en: Use AWS Glue to create an ML transform to build and train models. Use Amazon OpenSearch Service to visualize the data.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon SageMaker 构建和训练模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 可视化数据。
B.en: Use Amazon SageMaker to build and train models. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Marketplace 中预构建的 ML Amazon 系统映像 (AMI) 来构建和训练模型。使用 Amazon OpenSearch Service 可视化数据。
C.en: Use a pre-built ML Amazon Machine Image (AMI) from the AWS Marketplace to build and train models. Use Amazon OpenSearch Service to visualize the data.

D.zh: 使用 Amazon QuickSight 通过计算字段构建和训练模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 可视化数据。
D.en: Use Amazon QuickSight to build and train models by using calculated fields. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Glue 主要用于 ETL 任务，并不是专门为构建和训练机器学习模型而设计的。虽然AWS Glue具有ML转换功能，但其用途有限，无法满足复杂的机器学习需求。错误：Amazon OpenSearch Service 主要用于搜索和分析数据，并不是专门为商业智能可视化而设计的。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Glue is primarily used for ETL tasks and is not specifically designed for building and training machine learning models. Although AWS Glue has ML conversion capabilities, its uses are limited and cannot meet complex machine learning needs. Wrong: Amazon OpenSearch Service is primarily used for searching and analyzing data and is not specifically designed for business intelligence visualization.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon SageMaker 是 AWS 专门设计的机器学习服务，可以高效构建、训练和部署模型，以满足复杂的机器学习需求。正确：Amazon QuickSight 是 AWS 推出的一款商业智能工具，可以直接集成数据并进行可视化分析，适合在商业智能仪表板中使用。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon SageMaker is a machine learning service specially designed by AWS that can efficiently build, train, and deploy models to meet complex machine learning needs. Correct: Amazon QuickSight is a business intelligence tool from AWS that can directly integrate data and perform visual analysis, and is suitable for use in business intelligence dashboards.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Marketplace预建的AMI虽然可以快速部署机器学习环境，但需要用户主动管理和维护，增加了运营开销。错误：Amazon OpenSearch Service 不适合商业智能可视化。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although the pre-built AMI of AWS Marketplace can quickly deploy a machine learning environment, it requires user active management and maintenance, which increases operational overhead. Error: Amazon OpenSearch Service is not suitable for business intelligence visualization.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon QuickSight 是一种数据可视化工具，不适合构建和训练机器单词学习模型。正确：Amazon QuickSight 可用于数据可视化，但无法执行机器学习任务。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon QuickSight is a data visualization tool and is not suitable for building and training machine word learning models. Correct: Amazon QuickSight can be used for data visualization, but it cannot perform machine learning tasks.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 Amazon SageMaker 构建和训练模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 可视化数据。
answer_text.en: B. Use Amazon SageMaker to build and train models. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon SageMaker 是 AWS 专门设计的机器学习服务，可以高效构建、训练和部署模型，以满足复杂的机器学习需求。正确：Amazon QuickSight 是 AWS 推出的一款商业智能工具，可以直接集成数据并进行可视化分析，适合在商业智能仪表板中使用。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon SageMaker is a machine learning service specially designed by AWS that can efficiently build, train, and deploy models to meet complex machine learning needs. Correct: Amazon QuickSight is a business intelligence tool from AWS that can directly integrate data and perform visual analysis, and is suitable for use in business intelligence dashboards.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在多个 AWS 账户中运行其生产和非生产环境工作负载。这些账户位于 AWS Organizations 中的组织中。该公司需要设计一种解决方案来防止成本使用标签被修改。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is running its production and nonproduction environment workloads in multiple AWS accounts. The accounts are in an organization in AWS Organizations. The company needs to design a solution that will prevent the modification of cost usage tags. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 创建自定义 AWS Config 规则以防止除授权主体之外的标签修改。
A.en: Create a custom AWS Config rule to prevent tag modification except by authorized principals.

B.zh: 在 AWS CloudTrail 中创建自定义跟踪以防止标签修改。
B.en: Create a custom trail in AWS CloudTrail to prevent tag modification.

C.zh: 创建服务控制策略 (SCP) 以防止除授权主体之外的标签修改。
C.en: Create a service control policy (SCP) to prevent tag modification except by authorized principals.

D.zh: 创建自定义 Amazon CloudWatch 日志以防止标签被修改。
D.en: Create custom Amazon CloudWatch logs to prevent tag modification.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Config主要用于资源合规性检查和记录资源配置更改，但不能直接阻止操作（例如标签修改）。它可以检测违规行为，但无法主动阻止修改。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Config is mainly used for resource compliance checking and recording resource configuration changes, but it cannot directly prevent operations (such as label modifications). It can detect violations but cannot proactively prevent modifications.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS CloudTrail用于记录API调用和活动日志以帮助审核和控制，但它不能直接阻止操作（例如标签修改）。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS CloudTrail is used to record API calls and activity logs to help with auditing and control, but it cannot directly block operations (such as label modifications).

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：SCP 是 AWS Organizations 中的一项策略，可以限制或允许组中账户的操作。通过SCP，可以明确限制标记修改操作，以确保只有授权主体才能执行此操作。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: SCP is a policy in AWS Organizations that can limit or allow the operations of accounts in a group. Through SCP, mark modification operations can be explicitly restricted to ensure that only authorized subjects can perform this operation.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：CloudWatch Logs 用于控制和记录日志数据，但不能直接阻止阻塞操作（例如标签修改）。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: CloudWatch Logs is used to control and record log data, but it cannot directly block blocking operations (such as label modification).

answer_text.zh: C. 创建服务控制策略 (SCP) 以防止除授权主体之外的标签修改。
answer_text.en: C. Create a service control policy (SCP) to prevent tag modification except by authorized principals.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：SCP 是 AWS Organizations 中的一项策略，可以限制或允许组中账户的操作。通过SCP，可以明确限制标记修改操作，以确保只有授权主体才能执行此操作。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: SCP is a policy in AWS Organizations that can limit or allow the operations of accounts in a group. Through SCP, mark modification operations can be explicitly restricted to ensure that only authorized subjects can perform this operation.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司将其应用程序托管在 AWS 云中。该应用程序在 Auto Scaling 组中的 Elastic Load Balancer 后面的 Amazon EC2 实例上运行，并使用 Amazon DynamoDB 表。该公司希望确保该应用程序可以在另一个 AWS 区域中使用，并且停机时间最短。解决方案架构师应该如何做才能以最少的停机时间满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company hosts its application in the AWS Cloud. The application runs on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Elastic Load Balancer in an Auto Scaling group and with an Amazon DynamoDB table. The company wants to ensure the application can be made available in anotherAWS Region with minimal downtime. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements with the LEAST amount of downtime?

A.zh: 在灾难恢复区域中创建 Auto Scaling 组和负载均衡器。将 DynamoDB 表配置为全局表。配置 DNS 故障转移以指向新的灾难恢复区域的负载均衡器。
A.en: Create an Auto Scaling group and a load balancer in the disaster recovery Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

B.zh: 创建 AWS CloudFormation 模板以创建 EC2 实例、负载均衡器和需要时启动的 DynamoDB 表 配置 DNS 故障转移以指向新的灾难恢复区域的负载均衡器。
B.en: Create an AWS CloudFormation template to create EC2 instances, load balancers, and DynamoDB tables to be launched when needed Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

C.zh: 创建 AWS CloudFormation 模板以创建 EC2 实例和需要时启动的负载均衡器。将 DynamoDB 表配置为全局表。配置 DNS 故障转移以指向新的灾难恢复区域的负载均衡器。
C.en: Create an AWS CloudFormation template to create EC2 instances and a load balancer to be launched when needed. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

D.zh: 在灾难恢复区域中创建 Auto Scaling 组和负载均衡器。将 DynamoDB 表配置为全局表。创建 Amazon CloudWatch 警报以触发 AWS Lambda 函数，该函数更新指向灾难恢复负载均衡器的 Amazon Route 53。
D.en: Create an Auto Scaling group and load balancer in the disaster recovery Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to trigger an AWS Lambda function that updates Amazon Route 53 pointing to the disaster recovery load balancer.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：分析：在容灾区域创建Auto Scaling组和负载均衡器可以保证该区域的应用能够快速启动和运行。将DynamoDB表配置为全局表，可以实现跨区域数据同步，保证数据一致性。通过配置 DNS 故障转移，您可以在主区域不可用时快速切换到灾难恢复区域。此选项满足问题要求并提供最短的停机时间。正确的。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Analysis: Creating Auto Scaling groups and load balancers in the disaster recovery area can ensure that applications can start and run quickly in this area. Configuring DynamoDB tables as global tables can achieve cross-regional data synchronization and ensure data consistency. Configuring DNS failover allows you to quickly switch to the disaster recovery zone if the primary zone becomes unavailable. This option meets the question requirements and provides minimal downtime. correct.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：分析：使用CloudFormation模板可以快速部署资源，但灾难发生时需要手动启动资源，可能会导致长时间停机。如果不提前配置DynamoDB全局表，可能会出现数据同步问题。此选项不满足最短停机时间要求。错误。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Using CloudFormation templates you can quickly deploy resources, but you need to manually start the resources when a disaster occurs, which may lead to long downtime. If DynamoDB global tables are not configured in advance, data synchronization problems may occur. This option does not meet the Minimum Downtime requirement. mistake.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：分析：虽然配置了DynamoDB全局表，但在发生灾难时使用CloudFormation模板启动资源仍然会导致长时间的停机。此选项不满足最短停机时间要求。错误。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Although DynamoDB global tables are configured, using CloudFormation templates to start resources in the event of a disaster still results in long downtime. This option does not meet the Minimum Downtime requirement. mistake.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：分析：创建 Auto Scaling 组和负载均衡器以及配置 DynamoDB 全局表是正确的步骤。使用 CloudWatch 和 Lambda 函数更新 Route 53 的 DNS 记录是一种自动化方法，但比直接配置 DNS 故障转移更复杂，并且可能会增加延迟。此选项不满足最短停机时间要求。错误。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Creating the Auto Scaling group and load balancer and configuring DynamoDB global tables are the correct steps. Using CloudWatch and Lambda functions to update Route 53's DNS records is an automated approach, but is more complex than directly configuring DNS failover and may increase latency. This option does not meet the Minimum Downtime requirement. mistake.

answer_text.zh: A. 在灾难恢复区域中创建 Auto Scaling 组和负载均衡器。将 DynamoDB 表配置为全局表。配置 DNS 故障转移以指向新的灾难恢复区域的负载均衡器。
answer_text.en: A. Create an Auto Scaling group and a load balancer in the disaster recovery Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (58%) C (37%) 5%。热门讨论与考点摘要：分析：在容灾区域创建Auto Scaling组和负载均衡器可以保证该区域的应用能够快速启动和运行。将DynamoDB表配置为全局表，可以实现跨区域数据同步，保证数据一致性。通过配置 DNS 故障转移，您可以在主区域不可用时快速切换到灾难恢复区域。此选项满足问题要求并提供最短的停机时间。正确的。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (58%) C (37%) 5%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Analysis: Creating Auto Scaling groups and load balancers in the disaster recovery area can ensure that applications can start and run quickly in this area. Configuring DynamoDB tables as global tables can achieve cross-regional data synchronization and ensure data consistency. Configuring DNS failover allows you to quickly switch to the disaster recovery zone if the primary zone becomes unavailable. This option meets the question requirements and provides minimal downtime. correct.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要在 2 周内将 MySQL 数据库从其本地数据中心迁移到 AWS。该数据库大小为 20 TB。该公司希望以最短的停机时间完成迁移。哪种解决方案能够最经济有效地迁移数据库？
question_text.en: A company needs to migrate a MySQL database from its on-premises data center to AWS within 2 weeks. The database is 20 TB in size. The company wants to complete the migration with minimal downtime. Which solution will migrate the database MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 订购 AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 设备。将 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 与 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 结合使用来迁移数据库并复制持续更改。将 Snowball Edge 设备发送到 AWS 以完成迁移并继续正在进行的复制。
A.en: Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with replication of ongoing changes. Send the Snowball Edge device to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication.

B.zh: 订购 AWS Snowmobile 车辆。将 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 与 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 结合使用来迁移正在进行更改的数据库。将 Snowmobile 车辆发送回 AWS 以完成迁移并继续正在进行的复制。
B.en: Order an AWS Snowmobile vehicle. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with ongoing changes. Send the Snowmobile vehicle back to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication.

C.zh: 订购使用 GPU 进行计算优化的 AWS Snowball Edge 设备。将 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 与 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 结合使用来迁移正在进行更改的数据库。将 Snowball 设备发送到 AWS 以完成迁移并继续正在进行的复制
C.en: Order an AWS Snowball Edge Compute Optimized with GPU device. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with ongoing changes. Send the Snowball device to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication

D.zh: 订购 1 GB 专用 AWS Direct Connect 连接以建立与数据中心的连接。将 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 与 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 结合使用来迁移数据库并复制持续更改。
D.en: Order a 1 GB dedicated AWS Direct Connect connection to establish a connection with the data center. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with replication of ongoing changes.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：分析：此选项使用 AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 设备进行迁移，结合 AWS DMS 和 AWS SCT 进行数据库迁移和连续复制。 Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 专为大规模数据迁移而设计，可高效且经济高效地传输高达 20 TB 的数据。该方法适用于需要快速迁移且网络带宽有限的场景。正确性：正确。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Analysis: This option uses an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device for migration, combining AWS DMS and AWS SCT for database migration and continuous replication. Snowball Edge Storage Optimized is designed for large-scale data migration, enabling the efficient and cost-effective transfer of up to 20 TB of data. This method is suitable for scenarios where rapid migration is required and network bandwidth is limited. Correctness: Correct.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：分析：AWS Snowmobile是一种迁移PB级数据的解决方案，适合超大规模的数据迁移。 20TB的数据量远远小于Snowmobile的设计容量，使用Snowmobile会造成资源浪费和更高的成本。正确性：错误。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: AWS Snowmobile is a solution for migrating petabytes of data, suitable for very large-scale data migrations. The data volume of 20 TB is far less than the Snowmobile's design capacity, and using Snowmobile will result in a waste of resources and higher costs. Correctness: False.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：分析：Snowball Edge Compute Optimized 设备主要用于需要计算能力的场景（例如机器学习或视觉处理），并非为数据迁移而设计。此选项不满足问题要求，并且成本高于存储优化设备。正确性：错误。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Snowball Edge Compute Optimized devices are primarily used for scenarios that require computing power (such as machine learning or visual processing) and are not designed for data migration. This option does not meet the question requirements and costs more than a Storage Optimized device. Correctness: False.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：分析：专线网络提供稳定的网络连接，但迁移20TB的数据可能需要很长时间，特别是要求在两周内完成迁移。这种方法可能无法满足时间要求并且成本较高。正确性：错误。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Direct Connect provides a stable network connection, but migrating 20 TB of data may take a long time, especially when the migration is required to be completed within two weeks. This method may not meet time requirements and be costly. Correctness: False.

Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with replication of ongoing changes. Send the Snowball Edge device to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication.
